entities.name: CISPRIMERTOOL
entities.description: Software tool for the identification of conserved intron scanning regions using expressed sequence tag alignments to a model crop genome

entities.name: COMPARATIVE GENOMICS
entities.description: A strategy used to extend genomic sequence information from model organisms to understudied orphan crops

entities.name: CONSERVED INTRON SCANNING MARKERS
entities.description: Markers developed using a comparative genomics strategy for conserved intron scanning

entities.name: MODEL ORGANISMS
entities.description: Organisms with complete, annotated genomic sequence information used as a resource for other species

entities.name: ORPHAN CROPS
entities.description: Understudied crops that benefit from comparative genomic approaches

entities.name: EXPRESSED SEQUENCE TAG ALIGNMENTS
entities.description: The process of aligning expressed sequence tags to a sequenced model crop genome to identify conserved regions

entities.name: CONSERVED INTRON SCANNING PRIMERS
entities.description: Primers called CISP located within conserved exons near exon-intron boundaries

entities.name: ONION
entities.description: A crop used in alignments with rice to assess conserved intron scanning primers

entities.name: BANANA
entities.description: A crop used in alignments with rice to assess conserved intron scanning primers

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A crop used in alignments with rice to assess conserved intron scanning primers

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A crop used in alignments with rice to assess conserved intron scanning primers

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A model crop genome used for alignments with other crops to identify conserved regions

entities.name: EXONS
entities.description: Relatively conserved genomic regions where CISP are located

entities.name: EXON-INTRON BOUNDARIES
entities.description: Specific locations within exons where conserved intron scanning primers are positioned
